user.title: Climate Group Helper
user.description: Create a virtual controller that synchronizes multiple heating/cooling units. This group acts as a single smart entity in your dashboard and automations, consolidating states and commands.

data_description.name: A friendly name for your group (e.g. 'Living Room' or 'Whole House').
data_description.entities: Select the climate devices (thermostats, ACs) to include in this group.

error.no_entities: You must select at least one climate device.
error.invalid_entity: One or more selected devices are not valid climate entities.
error.duplicate_name: A climate group with this name already exists.
error.unavailable_entities: Some selected devices are unavailable. The group will be created but may not work fully until they respond.

members_section.name: Members & Modes
members_section.description: Add climate devices. Choose how different capabilities (presets, fan modes) are merged when members vary.

data_description.entities: Add or remove devices. Removing devices stops them from being controlled by the group.
data_description.master_entity: Designate one device as the leading thermostat. Its values can override group calculations.
data_description.hvac_mode_strategy: Strategy for calculating the group's state from its members (e.g. if one is 'heat' and another is 'off').
data_description.feature_strategy: Determines which features (e.g. preset modes, fan modes) are available on the group when members have different capabilities.
data_description.union_out_of_bounds_action: What to do with members that cannot reach the target temperature. Only applies when Feature Strategy is set to Union.

temperature_section.name: Temperature Settings
temperature_section.description: Calculate temperature from members or use master. External sensors and TRV calibration.

data_description.temp_target_avg: How the group calculates the target setpoint to send to its members.
data_description.temp_use_master: Use the master entity's setpoint instead of calculating an average. Falls back to averaging if unavailable.
data_description.temp_target_round: Only applies to values sent to devices, not to current/displayed values.
data_description.temp_current_avg: How the group summarizes sensor readings from its members.
data_description.temp_sensors: Use external sensors instead of member readings. These become the sole source of truth for the group's temperature.
data_description.temp_update_targets: Write the external sensor value to number entities (e.g. TRV calibration offsets). Useful for keeping thermostats aware of actual room temperature.
data_description.temp_calibration_mode: Absolute: Send value directly. Offset: Send delta (External - TRV Internal). Scaled: Send as Integer x100 (e.g. 21.5 -> 2150).
data_description.calibration_heartbeat: Periodically force-sync calibration to entities even if temperature hasn't changed. Set to 0 to disable.
data_description.calibration_ignore_off: Do not send calibration updates to members that are currently off.

humidity_section.name: Humidity Settings
humidity_section.description: Calculate humidity from members or use master. Optional external sensors.

data_description.humidity_target_avg: How the group calculates the target setpoint to send to its members.
data_description.humidity_use_master: Use the master entity's setpoint instead of calculating an average. Falls back to averaging if unavailable.
data_description.humidity_target_round: Only applies to values sent to devices, not to current/displayed values.
data_description.humidity_current_avg: How the group summarizes sensor readings from its members.
data_description.humidity_sensors: Use external sensors instead of member readings. These become the sole source of truth for the group's humidity.
data_description.humidity_update_targets: Write the external sensor value to number entities. Useful for dehumidifier calibration.

sync_section.name: Sync Mode
sync_section.description: Defines what happens when a member device is changed directly (e.g. via its own app or physical buttons) — not when you control the group itself. Choose which attributes are enforced.

data_description.sync_mode: What to do when a member is changed outside the group. Disabled: ignore it. Mirror: apply the change to all members. Lock: revert the member back to the group target. Master/Lock: only the master entity's changes are accepted.
data_description.sync_attributes: Attributes selected here will be enforced in Lock/Mirror modes. Unselected attributes allow for local control (e.g. sync temperature but let users change fan speed locally).
data_description.ignore_off_members_sync: When enforcing Lock/Mirror, skip members that are currently Off. Note: direct group commands always reach all capable members regardless of this setting.

window_section.name: Window Control
window_section.description: Turn off or set individual temperature when windows open. Restore when closed.

data_description.window_mode: Enable or disable climate group based on window/door sensors. When enabled, HVAC mode sets to 'off' when windows open and restores to the previous state when closed.
data_description.window_adopt_manual_changes: Allow target state changes while windows are open.
data_description.window_action: What to do when window opens: Turn Off completely or set a specific Temperature (e.g. frost protection).
data_description.window_temperature: Target temperature to set when 'Temperature' action is selected.
data_description.room_sensor: (Optional) Binary sensor for the room's own window/door.
data_description.zone_sensor: (Optional) Binary sensor for windows/doors in the zone/floor. Zone sensor should include room sensor.
data_description.room_open_delay: Time to wait after room window opens before turning off heating.
data_description.zone_open_delay: Time to wait after zone window opens before turning off heating.
data_description.close_delay: Time to wait after all windows close before restoring heating.

presence_section.name: Presence Control
presence_section.description: Manage climate settings based on room presence. After absence is detected for the configured duration, the away action is triggered. Previous settings are restored upon return.

data_description.presence_mode: Enable or disable automatic away-fallback when absence is detected.
data_description.presence_sensor: The entity reporting room presence. State 'on' or 'home' is treated as present.
data_description.presence_action: The fallback action to perform when absence is detected.
data_description.presence_away_offset: Offset from current target when away (e.g. -2° or +2°). Schedule changes during absence are applied automatically.
data_description.presence_away_temperature: Fixed temperature to set when away.
data_description.presence_away_preset: Preset mode to activate when away.
data_description.presence_away_delay: Wait time after sensor reports absence before activating away mode.
data_description.presence_return_delay: Wait time after sensor reports presence before restoring.

schedule_section.name: Schedule Automation
schedule_section.description: Connect HA schedule to automate temperatures. Set resync frequency and override duration. You can also use the set_schedule_entity service to switch schedules dynamically.

data_description.schedule_entity: Select a schedule entity. The schedule's 'Additional data' field can contain YAML with attributes like 'temperature: 20.5' or 'hvac_mode: heat'.
data_description.resync_interval: Force-sync members to the target state every X minutes. Ensures devices stay on schedule even if adjusted manually or commands are missed. Set to 0 to disable.
data_description.override_duration: The duration for manual overrides. After this time, the group automatically reverts to the current schedule. Set to 0 to skip the override period.
data_description.persist_changes: Changes to the schedule itself will be ignored while a manual override is active. Re-applies the current schedule only after the override expires.
data_description.persist_active_schedule: Retain the active schedule after a restart when changed via service. Otherwise, it resets to the default.
data_description.ignore_off_members_schedule: The Schedule will NOT turn on members that are currently Off.

temp_offsets_section.name: Member Offsets
temp_offsets_section.description: Set a temperature offset for individual members. The group target is adjusted by this value before sending to each device. Leave at 0 for no offset.

isolation_section.name: Member Isolation
isolation_section.description: Temporarily isolate specific members (e.g. radiators behind curtains) when a trigger is active. Isolated members are turned off and excluded from group calculations.

data_description.isolation_trigger: Choose what activates the isolation: a binary sensor or a specific HVAC mode of the group.
data_description.isolation_sensor: (Sensor trigger) Binary sensor that triggers isolation when ON.
data_description.isolation_trigger_hvac_modes: (HVAC mode trigger) Isolation activates when the group's target mode is one of these.
data_description.isolation_entities: Group members to isolate when the trigger is active.
data_description.isolation_activate_delay: (Sensor / HVAC Mode trigger only) Time to wait after the trigger activates before isolating members.
data_description.isolation_restore_delay: (Sensor / HVAC Mode trigger only) Time to wait after the trigger deactivates before restoring members to the group target state.

advanced_section.name: Advanced Settings
advanced_section.description: Debounce, retry behavior, debug sensors, and minimum temperature when off (force valve close).

data_description.debounce_delay: Time to wait before sending commands. Higher values prevent 'rapid-fire' commands when sliding controls, but feel slower.
data_description.retry_attempts: How many times to resend a command if a device doesn't respond. Set to 0 to disable.
data_description.retry_delay: Pause between retry attempts. Giving devices a moment to recover can improve reliability.
data_description.min_temp_off: Sends the device's minimum temperature (e.g. 5°C) when turning the group off. This ensures valves are fully closed for frost protection.
data_description.expose_smart_sensors: Creates extra sensor entities for the group's temperature and humidity (useful for graphs).
data_description.expose_member_entities: Creates an attribute listing all member entity IDs.
data_description.expose_config: Adds all configuration values to the entity attributes. Useful for debugging.

error.no_entities: At least one climate device must remain in the group.
error.calibration_requires_sensors: Calibration targets require at least one external temperature sensor.
error.master_refresh_notice: Master Entity added. New options available.
error.isolation_all_selected: At least one member must remain outside isolation.

options.normal: Normal – The group adopts the most common member mode
options.off_priority: Off Priority – If any member is Off, the whole group is Off
options.auto: Auto – Smart switching between Normal and Off Priority

options.intersection: Intersection – Support features only if ALL members have them
options.union: Union – Support all features present on ANY member

options.disabled: Disabled – No automatic sync between members
options.mirror: Mirror – Manual changes on one device update all members
options.lock: Lock – Reverts manual changes to match the group state
options.master_lock: Master/Lock – Only the master entity can change the group target

options.master_only: Master Only – Accept only the master entity

options.sensor: Binary Sensor – Isolate when sensor is ON
options.hvac_mode: HVAC Mode – Isolate when group mode matches
options.member_off: Member Off – Isolate when a member turns off manually

set_temperature.name: Set Temperature
set_temperature.description: Set the target temperature for the entire group.

set_schedule_entity.name: Set Schedule Entity
set_schedule_entity.description: Changes the active schedule entity used by the Climate Group.

schedule_entity.name: Schedule Entity
schedule_entity.description: The new schedule entity to use (e.g. schedule.holiday). Set to null or empty to restore the configured default schedule.

boost.name: Boost
boost.description: Temporarily sets the group to a target temperature for a specified duration. After the timer expires, the group reverts to the active schedule or its previous state. Specify either temperature or temperature_offset.

temperature.name: Temperature
temperature.description: Absolute target temperature during the boost (e.g. 24.0). Mutually exclusive with temperature_offset.

temperature_offset.name: Temperature Offset
temperature_offset.description: Relative offset added to the current target temperature (e.g. +3.0 or -2.0). Mutually exclusive with temperature.

duration.name: Duration (minutes)
duration.description: How long the boost should last in minutes.

member_unavailable.title: Member Device Unavailable
member_unavailable.description:
One or more devices in {group_name} are offline. The group will keep working with what it has.

Unavailable: {unavailable_entities}

no_members_available.title: No Members Available
no_members_available.description:
Group {group_name} has no active members and is effectively offline.

Check: {member_entities}

conflicting_features.title: Feature Conflicts
conflicting_features.description:
Group {group_name} has members with different features. Functionality might be limited to what everyone supports.

Conflicting: {conflicting_entities}
